skill.name: jira-safe
skill.description: Create SAFe-style Jira epics, stories, and subtasks with Jira Cloud hierarchy patterns for team-managed projects.
skill.summary: Plan and create SAFe Jira work items with parent-child linking, acceptance criteria, and workflow guidance.

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, credential, and heuristic warnings. Review confirms legitimate Jira automation with authenticated network writes and local credential loading, but no evidence of prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, arbitrary shell execution, or weak cryptography. Publish with warnings because the scripts can modify live Jira projects and the documentation includes a destructive delete helper.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Jira Issue Creation Scripts
medium_findings.description: The included Node.js and Python scripts read Jira credentials and send authenticated POST requests to create epics, stories, and subtasks. This is expected for a Jira automation skill, but it can modify a live Jira project when run with valid credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly build Basic authentication from Jira environment variables and call Jira REST API endpoints. The behavior is consistent with the skill purpose, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Local Credential File Loading
medium_findings.description: Python scripts load a parent-directory .env file and read Jira email, API token, base URL, and project key. This is a legitimate configuration pattern, but it increases exposure if users store broad Jira tokens in local files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env loading and os.environ access are explicit. I did not find evidence that the scripts send credentials anywhere except the configured Jira base URL.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Jira Delete Helper in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The main skill documentation includes a bulk delete helper that searches project issues and deletes each issue with subtasks. It is documentation, not an installed executable script, but copying it into a session could delete live project data without an approval guard.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented code uses DELETE against Jira issue endpoints inside a loop. It is not automatically executed by the skill files, which lowers confidence from critical to medium.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code fences, JavaScript template literals, shell examples, or console output. I found no evidence of child_process, subprocess, eval, or shell execution in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is used for formatting, Basic auth construction, and JavaScript string interpolation. No actual shell execution API appeared in the reviewed executable sections.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Certificate Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner appears to match words such as SAFe, Epic key, parent key, and issue key. I found no evidence of cryptographic operations or certificate file access in the cited sections.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines concern Jira issue keys or SAFe methodology text, not crypto primitives or key files. This strongly supports a false-positive assessment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated REST Writes to Jira
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts issue authenticated POST requests to Jira Cloud endpoints using locally supplied credentials. Users should run them only against intended projects with least-privilege tokens.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The REST writes are explicit and repeated across scripts. They are expected for Jira automation but still materially affect external state.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bulk Delete Example
dangerous_patterns.description: Documentation includes a Jira DELETE loop for all searched issues. This should require explicit user confirmation before use in any live project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The delete loop is clear, but it appears only as documentation. There is no evidence that it runs automatically.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose structure when turning product plans into Jira issues. This skill provides SAFe templates, hierarchy rules, and scripts for Jira Cloud work item creation.
actual_capabilities: Builds SAFe-style epics, features, stories, and subtasks for Jira Cloud.
Shows parent-child linking patterns for team-managed and company-managed Jira projects.
Provides Atlassian Document Format examples for descriptions and acceptance criteria.
Includes Node.js and Python scripts for creating Jira issues from predefined backlog content.
Documents workflow transitions, comments, and implementation update patterns.
limitations: Requires Jira Cloud credentials and a valid project configuration.
Scripts can create live Jira issues and should be reviewed before execution.
Examples are tailored to specific SCRUM-style project settings.
Does not provide a universal field mapping for every Jira instance.

use_cases.title: Structure a Product Backlog
use_cases.description: Convert product plans into SAFe epics, stories, and subtasks with consistent acceptance criteria.

use_cases.title: Create Implementation Stories
use_cases.description: Prepare Jira stories that include technical subtasks, definitions of done, and parent links.

use_cases.title: Automate Jira Setup
use_cases.description: Use the included scripts as a starting point for repeatable Jira issue creation.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Story
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SAFe Jira story for this request: [describe the work]. Include summary, user story, acceptance criteria, and definition of done.

prompt_templates.title: Break Down a Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Break this feature into Jira stories and subtasks using SAFe structure: [feature description]. Show parent-child relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Jira Creation Payloads
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare Jira Cloud issue fields for a team-managed project. Use parent links and Atlassian Document Format for this backlog: [backlog details].

prompt_templates.title: Review Workflow Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Jira automation plan for SAFe hierarchy, transition safety, rate limiting, and approval checkpoints: [plan or script summary].

output_examples.input: Create a Jira story for password reset.
output_examples.output: A SAFe story with a user persona, business benefit, Given-When-Then acceptance criteria, definition of done, and suggested subtasks.

output_examples.input: Break an onboarding feature into Jira work items.
output: One epic summary with business outcome.
Several linked stories with acceptance criteria.
Subtasks for implementation, testing, and documentation.

output_examples.input: Plan workflow updates for completed implementation work.
output_examples.output: A transition plan that queries available Jira transitions, adds an implementation comment, and moves the issue to the target state.

best_practices: Use least-privilege Jira API tokens and keep them outside committed files.
Review generated issue lists before running scripts against a live Jira project.
Query project style and available transitions before creating or moving issues.
anti_patterns: Running demo scripts against production Jira projects without review.
Assuming custom field IDs are identical across Jira instances.
Using bulk delete examples without explicit approval and a backup plan.

faq.question: Does this skill create Jira issues automatically?
faq.answer: It includes scripts that can create issues when run with valid Jira credentials. Review the target project before execution.

faq.question: Does it support team-managed Jira projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation emphasizes parent field linking for team-managed Jira projects.

faq.question: Can I use it with company-managed Jira projects?
faq.answer: Yes, but you must confirm custom field IDs such as Epic Link before creating issues.

faq.question: Where are Jira credentials stored?
faq.answer: The scripts read environment variables. Some Python scripts can load a parent-directory .env file.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for production Jira projects?
faq.answer: It is publishable with warnings. Test in a sandbox project and use least-privilege API tokens.

faq.question: Does it include workflow transition guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to query transitions, move issues, and add implementation comments.
